pi_items.metadata_value: CDC FoodNet Annual Summary Data (real surveillance data)
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data created via SQL INSERT statements, not actual published CDC data
pi_items.source: Comparison of setup_task.ps1 data values against official CDC MMWR 2019 FoodNet report. Setup claims Enteritidis 1.76 per 100k but CDC reports 2.6 per 100k; claims Typhimurium 1.54 but CDC reports 1.3; claims Newport 0.97 but CDC reports 1.4. Values do not match published CDC data.

SQL CREATE TABLE statement in setup_task.ps1 lines 60-68. Column names: Year INTEGER, Serotype TEXT(50), Site TEXT(5), CaseCount INTEGER, IncidenceRate DOUBLE, AgeGroup TEXT(20), RecordID PRIMARY KEY. No Sex column exists; State is named Site.
Analysis of INSERT statements in setup_task.ps1. First data row is year 2014 (line 92), last is year 2020 (line 199). No data for 2010-2013.
SQL CREATE TABLE statement at line 60 of setup_task.ps1 and referenced throughout task.json
Confirmed in setup_task.ps1 line 52 and throughout task files

pi_items.metadata_value: 10 FoodNet surveillance sites: CA, CO, CT, GA, MD, MN, NM, NY, OR, TN
pi_items.verified_value: Matches official CDC FoodNet sites: California, Colorado, Connecticut, Georgia, Maryland, Minnesota, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, Tennessee
pi_items.source: CDC FoodNet About page confirms 10 surveillance sites covering approximately 16% of US population. Setup script documents all 10 states at lines 74-76.

pi_items.metadata_value: Enteritidis, Typhimurium, Newport, Javiana, Infantis, Heidelberg, Montevideo, Muenchen, Oranienburg, Thompson
pi_items.verified_value: Enteritidis, Typhimurium, Newport, Javiana, Infantis are confirmed as top serotypes from CDC FoodNet; others are valid Salmonella serotypes
pi_items.source: CDC MMWR 2019 FoodNet report confirms top 5 serotypes match. CDC surveillance documents confirm other serotypes as legitimate Salmonella serotypes. All serotypes present in setup_task.ps1 lines 80-89.

pi_items.verified_value: All records have 'All ages' value only, no age stratification despite AgeGroup column being created
pi_items.source: All 64 INSERT statements in setup_task.ps1 (lines 92-199) specify AgeGroup as 'All ages'. No stratification by age found.

pi_items.verified_value: Sex column does NOT exist in database schema
pi_items.source: SQL CREATE TABLE statement (lines 60-68) creates only 7 columns: RecordID, Year, Serotype, Site, CaseCount, IncidenceRate, AgeGroup. No Sex column.
Count of INSERT INTO SalmonellaCases statements in setup_task.ps1 lines 92-199

pi_items.metadata_value: Real CDC FoodNet Annual Summary data with exact published values
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data created programmatically. Setup script states 'Values are exact published incidence rates' but comparison to official CDC 2019 MMWR data shows values do NOT match published rates
pi_items.source: Setup script lines 6-7 claim 'Values are exact published incidence rates...NO random generation'. However, setup claims 14,122 total Salmonella cases in top 10 serotypes but CDC reported only 8,556 total cases. Individual rates also differ significantly (e.g., setup: Newport 0.97/100k vs CDC: 1.4/100k).

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic Salmonella surveillance database created via SQL INSERT statements, not actual published CDC FoodNet data as claimed. The database schema has only 6 data columns (Year, Serotype, Site, CaseCount, IncidenceRate, AgeGroup) - not the 7 columns claimed in metadata. The column 'Site' contains state abbreviations (CA, CO, CT, etc.) for the 10 official FoodNet surveillance states, but metadata incorrectly labels this as 'State'. There is no Sex column despite being listed in metadata. The dataset spans 2014-2020, not 2010-2020 as claimed. The 64 records contain Salmonella serotypes that match real CDC serotypes (Enteritidis, Typhimurium, Newport, Javiana, Infantis), but the specific numerical values (incidence rates and case counts) do not match published CDC 2019 data. All age group values are 'All ages' with no age stratification despite having an AgeGroup column.
